content.claim: Hungary's secondary education network comprises 880 schools designed to ensure proximity (public transport access within one hour) for all students, with dormitory places available for those unable to commute; however, this proximity guarantee applies to generic secondary schools, not specifically to geographically clustered six- and eight-year selective tracks.
content.context: Fresh live research through the School network planning lens
limitations: System-level accessibility claim (under one hour) is not differentiated by track type
No dedicated school-bus service exists; reliance on public transport varies by rural settlement infrastructure
Dormitory supply data noted as 'exceeding demand,' but capacity and location relative to six-/eight-year tracks not specified
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
